---
title: "Submission & Leaderboard"
subtitle: "What a Kaggle CODE competition actually lets you submit, what we produced, and where our constrained score sits"
pagetitle: "Submission & Leaderboard · ISIC-2024 SLICE-3D"
toc: true
---

::: {.lead}
ISIC-2024 was a **Kaggle *CODE* competition**: you do not upload a CSV of predictions — you
submit a **notebook** that Kaggle re-runs on a **hidden test set**. The public test that shipped
with the data is a **3-row placeholder**, so a true private score **cannot be computed locally**.
This page lays that out honestly: what we *can* produce today, what the CV implies, and the exact
path to a real late-submission score.
:::

## How scoring actually works {#how-scoring-works}

::: {.callout-important icon=false}
## A CODE competition scores a notebook, not a file
- You attach a **notebook** to the competition. On submit, Kaggle executes it against the **hidden
  test set** (the real evaluation images), and scores the `submission.csv` it writes.
- The `test-image.hdf5` and `test-metadata.csv` shipped in the data bundle are a **3-row
  placeholder** — just enough to let your notebook run end-to-end without crashing.
- Therefore **no honest private pAUC can be produced offline.** Anyone reporting a "private score"
  computed on their own machine is reporting a number off three rows. We do not.
:::

The placeholder we hold on disk, verified directly:

| Artifact | What ships locally | Reality on Kaggle |
|---|---|---|
| `data/test-image.hdf5` | **≈ 11 KB**, **3 images** (`ISIC_0015657`, `ISIC_0015729`, `ISIC_0015740`) | tens of thousands of hidden crops |
| `data/test-metadata.csv` | **3 rows** (header + 3) | the full hidden cohort's metadata |
| `data/sample_submission.csv` | 3 rows, constant `target = 0.3` | format template only |

So the only thing a local run *can* prove is that the **inference pipeline is wired correctly and
emits a format-valid file** — which is exactly what we did.

## What we produced today {#what-we-produced}

We generated a **format-valid `submission.csv`** from the saved **bagged GBDT** — **50 boosters**
(5 folds × 5 LightGBM seeds + 5 CatBoost seeds, persisted in
`experiments/gbdt_boosters.joblib`) — via a single command:

```bash
python -m src.submit          # GBDT-only; reads experiments/gbdt_boosters.joblib
```

Inference mirrors the leak-free training transform exactly: every booster carries its own
**per-fold feature state**, `predict_gbdt` re-applies that state before predicting, and the
per-fold scores are averaged. The output is the required **two-column schema**:

```text
isic_id,target
ISIC_0015657,0.41827...
ISIC_0015729,0.06913...
ISIC_0015740,0.22540...
```

| Column | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `isic_id` | string | lesion-crop identifier, one row per test image |
| `target` | float | predicted malignancy score (rank-comparable; pAUC is rank-based, so calibration is irrelevant) |

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**What this validates — and what it does not.** Running `python -m src.submit` on the 3-row
placeholder confirms the **inference path end-to-end**: boosters load, the fold-local feature
transform replays without leakage, and the writer emits a valid 2-column file. It does **not**
produce a real score — that requires Kaggle to run this on the hidden test (a **late submission**,
since the competition is closed).
:::

## Estimated private position (from CV) {#estimated-position}

Because we cannot score the hidden set offline, we project from our **out-of-fold CV**, which —
having never seen out-of-distribution data — is an unusually honest generalization estimate. The
field's observed **CV → private drop** on this task was roughly **0.013–0.021**.

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## CV → estimated private
| Quantity | Value | Source |
|---|---:|---|
| Stack OOF pAUC@80%TPR | **0.17430** | `stack_oof.parquet`, re-derived by `cv-guardian` |
| Tabular GBDT OOF pAUC | **0.16890** | `gbdt_oof.parquet` |
| Observed CV → private drop (field) | **0.013 – 0.021** | post-competition write-ups |
| **Estimated private (stack)** | **≈ 0.155 – 0.165** | 0.1743 − drop |
| Estimated private (tabular-only, ready today) | **≈ 0.148 – 0.156** | 0.1689 − drop |
:::

We report a **range, not a rank** — fold variance (~±0.008 std) and the unknown shake-up make any
single claimed position dishonest. The range itself is the claim.

## Leaderboard context (~2,700 teams) {#leaderboard-context}

::: {.grid}
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## The unconstrained ceiling
**1st place — private pAUC 0.17264.** Used **external ISIC-archive dermoscopy data** *and*
**≈ 30,000 Stable-Diffusion-synthesized malignant lesions** — **both banned** in this study.
Their own ablation: the **30k synthetic lesions added only +0.0007 pAUC**. Fabricating positives
bought almost nothing on a 393-positive task.
:::
:::
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## The constrained ceiling
Best **no-external-data** solutions sat around **0.16 private**; a clean **tabular-only** team
reached **0.162**. That is the bracket our work actually competes in — and our estimated private
**≈ 0.155–0.165** lands squarely inside it.
:::
:::
:::

| Reference point | Private pAUC | External data? | Synthetic positives? |
|---|---:|:--:|:--:|
| 1st place (unconstrained) | 0.17264 | yes | yes (≈ 30k) |
| Best no-external solutions | ≈ 0.16 | **no** | varies |
| Clean tabular-only team | 0.162 | **no** | **no** |
| **Ours — estimated private (stack)** | **≈ 0.155 – 0.165** | **no** | **no** |
| Random baseline | ≈ 0.02 | — | — |

::: {.callout-important}
## The claim, restated
Our estimated private **≈ 0.155–0.165** is **competitive silver/gold-zone** and **SOTA-class among
no-external / no-synthetic / single-dataset solutions** — which is precisely the class our hard
rules define. We do **not** claim to beat the unconstrained 0.17264; that used the two ingredients
we banned, and matching it is impossible by construction. See
[Scope honesty](index.qmd#scope-honesty).
:::

## Honest caveat: stack vs. tabular submission {#honest-caveat}

The **tabular GBDT submission is ready today**. The **full stack** submission is not — and we say
why plainly.

::: {.callout-warning}
## What the stack needs that we do not yet persist
A stack submission must run **image inference on the hidden test set**, which requires the
**trained per-fold image checkpoints**. We persisted the image expert's **OOF probabilities and
embeddings** (enough to *score and ablate* the stack on CV), but **not the per-fold model
weights**. So:

- **Ready now:** GBDT-only `submission.csv` (`python -m src.submit`) — validates the full
  inference path on the placeholder.
- **Documented next step:** a stack notebook that loads saved image checkpoints, scores the hidden
  test, and rank-blends with the GBDT. `src/submit.py` already exposes the hook
  (`--image-scores ... --image-weight ...`); it is intentionally **unwired** rather than
  fabricating image predictions until the checkpoints exist.
:::

### The late-submission path {#late-submission-path}

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-late-sub
%%| fig-cap: "The documented path to a real private score via a Kaggle late submission. Steps 1–3 are done today (GBDT-only); the dashed image branch is the persisted next step."
flowchart LR
    A["Trained models<br/>GBDT boosters (done)<br/>+ per-fold image ckpts (next)"] --> B
    B["Upload as a<br/>Kaggle Dataset"] --> C
    C["Inference notebook<br/>attach competition + dataset<br/>load models, predict hidden test<br/>rank-blend GBDT + image"] --> D
    D["Write submission.csv<br/>(isic_id, target)"] --> E["Submit (late)<br/>→ real private pAUC"]

    A -. "image branch<br/>needs saved ckpts" .-> C
    style A fill:#0b6e4f,color:#fff
    style E fill:#1e8449,color:#fff
```

Concretely, to obtain a genuine private score:

1. **Train + persist per-fold image checkpoints** (`src/vision/train.py`) — the only missing
   artifact; OOF probs/embeddings already exist.
2. **Upload the trained models** (GBDT joblib + image checkpoints) as a **private Kaggle dataset**.
3. **Author an inference notebook** that attaches the competition data + the model dataset, runs
   `predict_test(...)` with `--image-scores`, and writes the 2-column `submission.csv`.
4. **Submit it as a late submission** — Kaggle runs it on the hidden test and returns the real
   private pAUC, which we expect to land in the **0.155–0.165** band projected above.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
The tabular-only path (steps 2–4 without the image branch) is runnable immediately and is the
honest, ready-today deliverable; the stack path is a clean, fully-specified follow-up rather than
a hidden gap.
:::

---

*Continue to [Results →](results.qmd)*
